title: Slow-exhale body scan for financial anxiety
subtitle: A ten-minute body scan for releasing the physical grip of money anxiety.
excerpt: A ten-minute body scan for financial anxiety — the tension that money worry leaves in the body: the clenched jaw, the tight chest, the held breath. The scan moves from feet to head, pausing at each area for a slow out-breath. Use when the money anxiety has been in the body for most of the day and it's time to put it down.
sourceNotes: Body scan meditation is a public-domain practice documented in mindfulness traditions (Kabat-Zinn's MBSR body scan, 1979 onward) and clinical research. The money-anxiety application is synthesised from Days 2, 3, and 6 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use at the end of a high-money-anxiety day — when the tension has been in the body for hours and it needs a deliberate release before sleep. Also useful after a tapping session that cleared the mental loop but left some physical residue.

content.text: A ten-minute body scan for the physical grip of money anxiety — the clenched jaw, the tight chest, the shoulders that have been up since the banking app opened. The scan moves from feet to head, pausing at each area for a slow out-breath to release what has accumulated there. Use at the end of a high-anxiety day, or after tapping that cleared the mental loop but left tension in the body.

Lie on your back, arms loosely at the sides. Close your eyes. Three slow breaths to settle. You are not trying to solve anything in the next ten minutes — the scan is a noticing practice, not a fixing one.
Begin at the feet. Notice the soles, the toes, the arches. Is there tension here? One slow in-breath. One long, slow out-breath, letting whatever is there soften without forcing it.
Move to the calves and shins. One breath. Notice and release. Move to the knees, the thighs. One breath at each area. No hurry.
Arrive at the belly. This is where anxiety often lives — a held quality, a sense of bracing. One slow in-breath into the belly. Watch it rise. One long out-breath, letting it fall all the way.
Move to the chest. Money worry often sits here as a tightness behind the breastbone. One slow breath in. On the out-breath, feel the chest soften. One more breath if the tightness is persistent.
Shoulders. Jaw. Forehead. At each: one in-breath to notice, one long out-breath to soften. The jaw especially — let it hang slightly open on the out-breath. Financial anxiety lives in the jaw.

After the full scan: three slow breaths through the whole body at once. Feeling the whole body softer than it was ten minutes ago. Then open the eyes slowly. Lie still for thirty seconds before moving.

Body scan meditation is a public-domain practice documented in mindfulness traditions (Kabat-Zinn's MBSR body scan, 1979 onward) and clinical research on anxiety and stress. The money-anxiety application is synthesised from themes in Days 2, 3, and 6 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), which addresses the physical residue of money anxiety.
